rulings.Case Title: CLEM FITZHUGH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 5, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for plaintiff¿s alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant¿s Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff¿s objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. The parties have stipulated that the oral arguments made on February 26, 2014 in the hearings for Godbee, Schlimmer, Santa, Rieser and Bettencourt apply to this motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. on Wednesday, March 5, 2014. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EMIL DOLNER et al VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication;
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 5, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 3 (false representation and punitive damages) and denied as to issue 2 (loss of consortium). As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issue 2, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Dolner¿s deposition testimony and declaration and the declarations of Dr. Limpert and Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether Mr. Dolner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. As to summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages, such issues were unopposed by plaintiffs and defendant¿s undisputed material facts supporting those issues were not disputed. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Dolner¿s deposition testimony and declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to the Limpert and Ay declarations are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Joel Cohen are sustained. Defendant¿s objection to its 1998 standard interrogatory responses based on lack of verification is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: EMIL DOLNER et al VS. PNEUMO ABEX LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (Joinder) In Maremont Corp.S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 5, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Navistar, Inc.¿s joinder in Maremont Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied. Defendant¿s so-called joinder failed to include a separate statement in violation of C.C.P. 437c(b)(1). Defendant cannot join another party¿s summary motion. (Frazee v. Seely (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 627.) Defendant¿s joinder is also denied as untimely for failing to provide sufficient notice required by C.C.P. 437c(a). If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
